name: Jumbo Rocks Campground
location: Inside Joshua Tree National Park, high desert
highlights: 124 sites set among massive boulder formations
The most immersive camping experience in the park
Excellent dark sky conditions
First-come, first-served — no reservations
bookingWindow: No reservations — first-come, first-served. Arrive early on weekends, especially spring and fall.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No water — bring your own. Cold winter nights can drop below freezing.

name: Reset Hotel
location: 7000 Split Rock Ave, Twentynine Palms — 5 min from north park entrance
highlights: First new-build hotel in the Morongo Basin in 15 years
65 rooms on 180 acres — 170 acres left deliberately undeveloped
Every room has a private patio with Solo Stove fire pit and desert views
Cedar sauna, cold plunge, saltwater pool, Celestron telescope for stargazing
Designed around the question: what would a hotel on Mars look like, built in connection to nature
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead for weekends. Spring and fall fill fastest.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. Summer pool season April–October. Winter evenings ideal for stargazing with Celestron telescopes.

name: Hidden Valley Campground
location: Inside Joshua Tree National Park, high desert
highlights: 44 sites inside the iconic boulder enclosure
Smaller and more intimate than Jumbo Rocks
Legendary bouldering access
Fills fast on weekends
bookingWindow: No reservations — first-come, first-served. Arrive before noon on weekends.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No water. Adjacent to Hidden Valley Trail and bouldering areas.

name: Skylight Joshua Tree
location: Near the park's north edge, Twentynine Palms
highlights: Intentionally designed desert cabins and domes
Stargazing-optimized architecture
Off-grid feel with considered design
Outdoor soaking tubs and fire pits
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead for weekends. Easier midweek.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Spring and fall most comfortable. Summer requires AC units that some cabins have.

name: 29 Palms Inn
location: Twentynine Palms, near the north entrance
highlights: Historic oasis property since 1928
Adobe bungalows around a natural pool fed by underground spring
Gardens, resident animals, a genuinely timeless quality
One of the most special small lodges in the American West
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead. Popular with returning guests — some weekends fill months out.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The garden is especially beautiful in spring. On-site restaurant serves breakfast and dinner.

name: Sacred Sands
highlights: Boutique B&B — two rooms only
Thoughtfully appointed, meditation garden
Personal attention from the owners
360-degree desert views
bookingWindow: Only two rooms — book 1–2 months ahead. Sells out for full moons and spring weekends.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Full moon weekends are especially popular and book far ahead.

name: Two Bunch Palms
location: Desert Hot Springs, ~45 min from Joshua Tree
highlights: Historic mineral hot springs spa since 1930
Fed by a natural geothermal aquifer
Grotto pools, couples treatments, serious relaxation
Long a Hollywood hideaway — the counterpoint to a strenuous park day
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead. Weekend packages sell out in spring season.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Summer can be extremely hot — the springs are still a draw but the surrounding heat is intense.

name: Hotel Wren
location: Twentynine Palms — 10 min from north park entrance
highlights: Woman-owned, design-led 12-room hideaway in a reimagined 1940s roadside motel
No televisions — designed intentionally for presence and reflection
Hand-carved woodwork, custom millwork, bookshelves with astronomy and geology titles
Saltwater pool and hot tub, private patios, Windsong desert provisions bodega on-site
21+ property — a quiet, considered retreat
bookingWindow: Book 3–6 weeks ahead. Only 12 rooms — fills quickly for full moon weekends and spring.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. 21+ only. No televisions in rooms by design. Spring wildflower season is peak.

name: The Bungalows at Joshua Tree Retreat Center
location: 59700 29 Palms Highway, Joshua Tree — minutes from the park's west entrance
highlights: 14 mid-century suites on the historic Joshua Tree Retreat Center campus
Buildings designed by Lloyd Wright (Frank Lloyd Wright's son) using sacred geometry
Access to retreat center's meditation areas, pool, hot pool, and vegetarian café
On-site vortex energy sites — the reason this land was selected in the 1940s
Floor-to-ceiling windows, private patios, desert views in all directions
bookingWindow: Book through retreat.homesteadmodern.com. 2–4 weeks ahead for weekends. Full buyout available for groups.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. The vegetarian Food for Thought Café is on-site. Spring and fall most pleasant.

name: AutoCamp Joshua Tree
location: 62025 29 Palms Hwy, Joshua Tree — minutes from the park's west entrance
highlights: Polished Airstream trailers, modern cabins, and luxury tents on a design-forward desert campus — the lodging that put Joshua Tree glamping on the map
Each Airstream is a self-contained suite: queen bed, bathroom, kitchenette, private deck with fire pit and desert views
Pool, clubhouse, communal fire pits, and loaner bikes for riding into town. The common spaces are as considered as the rooms.
Now part of Hilton's portfolio but retains its original design identity and Airstream-specific experience — the brand didn't dilute the place
bookingWindow: Book 2-4 weeks ahead at autocamp.com. Weekends and spring fill fastest.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. Summer is hot — pool is essential. October through April is the sweet spot.
